The way that I'm going to implement that is that when the LLM wants to click on something, this is the way I decided to architect it and it works quite well for me: when the LLM wants to click on something, it doesn't output the coordinates but it outputs a description of what it wants to click on, and then we have a secondary LLM right here that converts query to coordinates.
Right, and so this secondary LLM is OS Atlas, so that smaller LLM which is 9B parameters gets called only when the reasoning LLM decides to use it.
